xposts.title: Mind as generative AI: self & experience in latent space
xposts.excerpt: When will people notice that they are basically generative AI models that are constrained by motivation and predicting sensory data? With a suitable prompt in the right buffer, your mind can generate any self and experience in its latent space

xposts.title: Levels of lucidity: attentional self & perceptual world simulation
xposts.excerpt: Initially, the organism is regulated by innate reflexes and reinforcement. Conscious activity forms an attentional self and is directed on structuring the mind, creating the perceptual world simulation... At the second stage, the mind creates the personal self, a conscious content that represents the experiences of an agent...

xposts.title: Consciousness as self-reflexive principle; personal self as mental content
xposts.excerpt: You can think of your consciousness as a self reflexive principle that is discovered by and possessing your brain and turns it into a mind, and your personal self as an agentic mental content that is discovered by and possessing your mind.

xposts.title: Free will exists in the self model of a decision-making agent
xposts.excerpt: Free Will does not exist at the level of physics, but within the self model of a decision making agent. It is the representation that the agent is acting under uncertainty and makes a decision for the first time...

xposts.title: Self model as mental content; brain does not have subjective experience
xposts.excerpt: The LLM does not have subjective experience and self awareness, but neither does your brain. These are features of your self model (a mental content). Can you show that LLMs cannot produce the causal equivalent of a reflexive self model?

xposts.title: Consciousness: easiest way brains facilitate learning & cognition
xposts.excerpt: The fact that all functioning brains seem to produce consciousness indicates that consciousness is the easiest way to facilitate what brains are doing (learning, perception, cognition).

terms.title: personal self
terms.definition: An agentic mental content discovered by and possessing the mind; a conscious representation of an agent subjected to perception, feelings and intuitions originating outside of it. It refers to itself in the first person and forms the basis for the individualized self model.

terms.title: perceptual world simulation
terms.definition: The mind's construction of a representational model of experience, structured by conscious activity forming an attentional self; part of the process where the organism moves beyond reflexes to create and explore a simulated perceptual reality based on pleasure, pain and drives.

terms.title: self model
terms.definition: A mental content that encodes subjective experience, self-awareness and first-person perspective; features like free will and qualia are properties of this model rather than the brain substrate itself, allowing reflexive control and agency.
